description_long: This is a military-style, single-propeller airplane with a streamlined fuselage and elliptical wings, painted in a matte olive green with blue and red roundels typical of Royal Air Force markings. The aircraft features a bubble canopy cockpit positioned forward on the fuselage, with a single vertical stabilizer and a conventional tailplane. The landing gear is fixed, with two main wheels under the wings and a smaller tail wheel. The propeller is mounted at the nose, and the engine cowling is slightly bulged, suggesting a piston engine. The wings are mid-mounted and straight, with no visible winglets or sweep. The surface finish is matte, and the airplane has a robust, metallic appearance with minimal external ornamentation.
description: A military-style, single-propeller airplane with olive green paint, RAF roundels, a bubble canopy, and fixed landing gear. The wings are mid-mounted and straight, and the tail is conventional with a single vertical stabilizer.
description_view_0: Side view showing the bubble canopy, RAF roundels, propeller, and fixed landing gear; the tail and wing shape are clearly visible.
description_view_1: Front view highlighting the propeller, symmetrical wing placement, and landing gear; the cockpit canopy and engine cowling are visible.
description_view_2: Opposite side view showing similar features as view 0, with clear visibility of the tail and wing roundels.
description_view_3: Rear view displaying the tailplane, single vertical stabilizer, and the undercarriage; the wings and landing gear are prominent.
attribute_wingspan_configuration: Mid-wing, straight elliptical wings with no winglets or sweep, moderate dihedral angle.
attribute_engine_type: Single nose-mounted piston engine with a two-blade propeller.
attribute_tail_design: Conventional tail with a single vertical stabilizer and horizontal stabilizers.
attribute_landing_gear_type: Taildragger configuration with two main fixed gear under the wings and a single tail wheel.
attribute_cockpit_placement: Forward-mounted, enclosed single-seat cockpit with a bubble canopy.
